meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Portfolio Building to Solve 'Can't Finish Projects'
content.seo_description: Stop starting and start shipping. Learn how to use portfolio-driven development with Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your SaaS MVP and generate revenue.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of shipping constraints. By pivoting from 'building an app' to 'building a portfolio of micro-SaaS features,' you create modular, sellable assets. Using Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you turn stalled codebases into high-velocity, revenue-generating products.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS founders is littered with 90% complete projects—brilliant ideas that stalled because of feature creep and technical debt. You aren't failing because you lack vision; you are failing because you lack a shipping mechanism. The secret isn't more discipline; it's a structural pivot from monolithic development to portfolio-driven architecture.

Imagine breaking your daunting roadmap into a series of high-impact, independent 'portfolio pieces' that can be deployed individually. By leveraging Antigravity to define your project constraints and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, you transform the overwhelming mountain of 'todo' into a series of quick, profitable wins.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP
steps.description: Identify the smallest piece of functionality that provides standalone value. This prevents scope creep and ensures your first portfolio piece is ready for market.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my core SaaS idea and suggest a 48-hour 'Atomic MVP' feature that provides immediate utility to users and can be marketed as a standalone micro-tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a directory structure for an atomic MVP focusing on user authentication and data storage.'

steps.title: Architect with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a robust scaffold that enforces modularity, ensuring each feature in your portfolio is decoupled from the main codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular project architecture that separates the UI, API, and database logic to ensure future portability of this portfolio piece.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the architectural patterns provided by Antigravity to my current project folder.'

steps.title: Automate Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Leverage Claude Code to rapidly write unit tests and core logic, ensuring that your MVP is not just built fast, but built to production standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic for the user dashboard, focusing on clean code, error handling, and performance optimization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Implement core business logic for dashboard; add unit tests for user authentication edge cases.'

steps.title: Implement Rapid Feedback Loops
steps.description: Integrate a feedback mechanism directly into your MVP to validate that your portfolio piece actually solves a user problem before building further.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a lightweight feedback collection component that triggers after a user completes a primary task in the MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create a feedback component that saves user input to a local JSON log.'

steps.title: Deploy and Iterate
steps.description: Ship your MVP to a public URL to start gathering real data, which dictates the direction of your next portfolio project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script and configuration file for a standard cloud platform to host this micro-SaaS MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate deployment scripts and verify all dependencies are locked for production.'

pro_tips: Always build your features as if they were micro-services; if you need to scrap the project, you can reuse the auth or payment module in your next venture.
Use Claude Code to review your own code for 'complexity smells' every 4 hours of development to maintain high velocity.
Market your 'in-progress' builds on Twitter/X to build a waitlist before the feature is even finished; this forces you to finish.
Don't build a database schema for the future; build for the next 7 days of user interaction only.
If a feature takes more than 3 days to build, it's not an atomic MVP—break it in half again.
content.next_steps: Once your first portfolio piece is live, gather user feedback for 72 hours. Use that feedback to decide if you should iterate on the current piece or pivot to the second piece in your portfolio roadmap.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting unfinished projects sit in your repo gathering digital dust. By treating your code as a portfolio of modular assets, you increase your chances of shipping a profitable product by 10x. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
